Design.Description: Primary vertical framing stud extending from bottom to top plates.
Full-height stud flanking window or door openings to carry loads (Same as Vertical Stud, but around opennings).
Short stud below a window opening that supports the bottom sill plate (If window).
Oriented-strand-board panel fastened to the frame for sheathing and bracing.
Horizontal member capping the wall and tying the studs together.
Shortened stud under the lef/right side of a kings supporting an opening (Shorter than kings length from the bottom plate to the header/bear loading).
Load-bearing blocking/header component distributing loads across an opening (Usually 2 elements with 2x10in cross section stacked).
Utility stud providing lateral bracing or service-run pathways (beside the first vertical stud).
Horizontal plate forming the sill at the bottom of a opening (If window).
Horizontal framing member anchored to the floor, forming the base of the wall.
Horizontal plate forming the upper sill of a window opening, providing a bearing surface for top cripples and tying the frame together only if there is no Bear Loading required in the openning.
Short vertical stud positioned above a header (Top Sill Plate) only if there is no Bear Loading required in the openning.
